pr.title: Match several ft, pl, and if functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Open decomp-matching PR that matched four functions across fighter common guard, player attack stats, and interface/UI code: ftCo_80092158, ftCo_80093790, plAttack_8003759C, and un_802FE260. It also improved fn_802FDA78 in if_2FC93 using better local struct access, signed byte sentinel typing, fieldwise color stores, const data, and stack/local-layout tuning. The decomp-dev GALE01 report shows matched code +2016 bytes and the four listed functions at 100.00%.

key_files.role: Matched ftCo_80092158 and ftCo_80093790 by replacing M2C color-table access and adjusting local/stack layout.
Matched un_802FE260 and improved fn_802FDA78 with file-local struct pointer access, signed x31 sentinel handling, const slot data, and fieldwise material diffuse writes.
Matched plAttack_8003759C by introducing static inline clearAttackStats and zeroing all attack-stat indices.

what_changed: ftCo_80092158 now computes a color-table offset as Player_GetUnk45(GET_FIGHTER(gobj)->player_id) << 2, indexes Fighter_804D650C through a color pointer, and passes packed RGB as (color[0] << 16) | (color[1] << 8) | color[2] to efSync_Spawn instead of M2C_FIELD/M2C_BITWISE temporaries.
ftCo_80093790 now uses an explicit 8-byte dummy local array, calls ftCo_80092158 directly with fp->parts[fp->ft_data->x8->x11].joint, then sets fp->x2219_b0 and calls ft_PlaySFX.
if_2FC93 changed un_804A1F10.x31 from unsigned char to signed char, using -1 as the sentinel instead of 0xFF.
if_2FC93 made un_804DDBA8 a static const char array and casts its element address to void* when assigning gobj->user_data.

fn_802FDA78 now takes struct un_804A1F10_t* p = &un_804A1F10, uses u8 j/k counters, writes GXColor diffuse components r/g/b/a field-by-field through HSD_MObj locals, uses 2.6f, and accesses x28/x30/x31/x32 through p.
un_802FE260 now uses the same p pointer for x24, x28, and x4 writes during the four UI GObj setup loop.
plattack.c added static inline clearAttackStats(struct plAttackStats* s) and calls it for stats->attacks, stats->hits, and stats->x358_hits.
plAttack_8003759C now loops from 0 to StatsAttack_Count for by_attack_counts and x504 resets, replacing prior loops that started at 2.
smart_moves: Replaced decompiler artifact macros in ftCo_80092158 with a real stride-4 color-table interpretation, improving both readability and matching.
Typed x31 as signed so the observed 0xFF sentinel can be expressed naturally as -1, avoiding unsigned compare/codegen friction.
Used a file-local pointer to the global un_804A1F10 state to shape repeated global field access in both fn_802FDA78 and un_802FE260.
Changed whole GXColor struct assignments into per-channel diffuse stores, a useful tactic when aggregate assignment emits the wrong store pattern.
Introduced a small static inline reset helper in plattack.c to deduplicate three identical plAttackStats clears while still matching the target code.
Used concrete dummy/local variables, including u8 _[8] in ftCo_80093790, instead of leaving broad M2C/PAD_STACK artifacts.
decomp_lessons: When a table index is later used as a byte offset, check for hidden struct/stride semantics; here Player_GetUnk45 was shifted by 2 before indexing Fighter_804D650C.
Byte fields holding 0xFF may really be signed sentinels; changing the field type can unlock cleaner comparisons and matching.
For small global state structs, introducing a local pointer to the static object can materially affect codegen and is reusable in similar file-local UI/state code.
Do not assume aggregate struct assignment is best for matching; fieldwise stores may be required for HSD material or GXColor writes.
Loop lower bounds are matching-sensitive. This PR changed reset loops from 2 to 0 for StatsAttack_Count arrays, so future work should verify decompiler-guessed bounds against expected codegen.
Const-qualifying small static data can help data matching, but user_data assignments may then need explicit casts.
Dummy stack locals are acceptable matching tools when evidenced by the diff, but should be kept narrow and documented by context.
naming_conventions: Existing unresolved symbol names were preserved for matched functions and globals: ftCo_80092158, ftCo_80093790, plAttack_8003759C, un_802FE260, fn_802FDA78, un_804A1F10, and un_804DDBA8.
The new helper uses lowerCamelCase descriptive naming: clearAttackStats.
Local names moved away from temp_ret/temp_r7/M2C-style names toward semantic names such as offset and color.
The file-local global pointer is named p in if_2FC93, matching a compact decomp style for repeated access to un_804A1F10.
The signed sentinel field remains structurally named x31, but comparisons now use -1 rather than 0xFF.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Color packing expression in ftCo_80092158: (color[0] << 16) | (color[1] << 8) | color[2].
Stack/local-layout padding in ftCo_80093790 via u8 _[8] = { 0 }.
Removed PAD_STACK(24) in fn_802FDA78 by replacing it with concrete scoped HSD_MObj* locals and explicit diffuse component stores.
Used u8 j and u8 k counters initialized before the switch in fn_802FDA78.
Used 2.6f instead of 2.6 in HSD_JObjSetTranslateY calculations to influence floating-point codegen.
Cast const slot-byte addresses with gobj->user_data = (void*) &un_804DDBA8[i].
Used static inline clearAttackStats so repeated reset logic could be expressed once while still inlining at the three callsites.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
decomp-dev[bot] GALE01 report: matched code 70.13% (+0.05%, +2016 bytes) and matched data 41.28% (+0.00%, +16 bytes).
decomp-dev[bot] reported 5 new matches: plAttack_8003759C, ftCo_80093790, ftCo_80092158, un_802FE260, and .sdata.
decomp-dev[bot] also reported fn_802FDA78 improved from 82.48% to 99.25%, plus improvements to several unmatched ftCo_Guard items and .sdata2.
follow_up_queries: Find other Fighter_804D650C uses that may need stride-4 color indexing and packed RGB construction.
Investigate the remaining fn_802FDA78 mismatch after this PR's 99.25% improvement, especially local order, signedness, and material store patterns.
Search for other plAttackStats reset code that could reuse or validate clearAttackStats-style inlining.
Audit un_804A1F10_t fields x24, x28, x2C, x30, x31, and x32 for better names once more interface/UI context is available.
Look for other 0xFF byte sentinels that should be signed char fields compared against -1.
